signals.title: USD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: NZD 1W avg: +0.503% (n=4) vs JPY 1W avg: -0.793% (n=7) — G10 spread: 1.296%. 275bp Fed-BoJ carry differential sustains USD/JPY bid; intervention risk on a break above 160.00. Carry dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +29,035 (bullish spec positioning) vs IV 17.3% and HV30 7.4% — VRP +9.9%. Elevated vol premium and net long positioning signal late-trend caution.

signals.title: EUR/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD COT LF net short -67,504 (specs short) vs ECB hike prob 0% priced for 23 Jul. Short-squeeze risk if ECB hikes or signals further tightening — crowded spec short unwind targets 1.15+.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD 25d RR at -0.80 — moderate put skew signals market biased for GBP weakness. However, GBP/USD IV 11.0% and HV30 5.4% — VRP +5.6% supports hedging demand.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: The NZD/USD carry disadvantage of -150bp (NZD 2.25% vs USD 3.75%) weighs on the pair, which currently trades at 0.5678. Despite this, specs remain short NZD −23.1k, positioning against the RBNZ's hawkish bias with 62% of a hike priced. This COT-OIS divergence sets up a short-squeeze risk, as the market is positioned for a dovish surprise, but the RBNZ's forward guidance points to further rate hikes. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 62% hike priced) on 8 Jul will be a key catalyst for this unwind.
